description:
You are a platform-engineering SRE doing routine housekeeping for a CI/CD workspace.

Current situation  
• All build artefacts are kept below the directory  
  /home/user/ci_cd/build-artifacts  
• A few “*.tmp” files that are no longer needed were accidentally committed and must be deleted.  
• A compliance rule says that every file you delete has to be written to a text-based deletion log the moment it is deleted. The auditors will machine-check that log, so its exact formatting matters.

Your assignment  
1. Use a single pipeline of <find> and <xargs> (at most two or three simple commands in total) to delete every file whose name ends with “.tmp” anywhere inside /home/user/ci_cd/build-artifacts.  
2. While deleting, simultaneously create the log file  
   /home/user/cleanup/deleted_tmp_files.log  
   The directory /home/user/cleanup may or may not exist yet, so handle that.  
3. Do NOT touch, move or list any files that do not end with “.tmp”.  
4. The log file must meet **all** of these formatting rules (the automated checker is strict):  
   • One absolute path per line.  
   • Paths must be sorted in ordinary ASCII/lexicographical order before being written.  
   • No blank lines anywhere in the file.  
   • No leading or trailing spaces on any line.  
   • A final terminating newline at the end of the file is allowed but not mandatory.  
5. When you are done, the “*.tmp” files should be gone and the log should list exactly the files you removed.

Hints you can rely on  
• You do not need sudo.  
• A typical pattern is: find … -print | sort | tee … | xargs …, but feel free to choose any equivalent that meets the constraints above.

The automated tests will verify both (a) that all and only the .tmp files were deleted and (b) that /home/user/cleanup/deleted_tmp_files.log exists and matches the formatting rules exactly. Good luck!
truth:
Initial on-disk state before the agent begins
--------------------------------------------
Directories:
  /home/user/ci_cd
  /home/user/ci_cd/build-artifacts
  /home/user/ci_cd/build-artifacts/cache

Files that MUST exist (and their initial contents, which are irrelevant to the task):
  /home/user/ci_cd/build-artifacts/temp1.tmp                 (text: “temp1”)
  /home/user/ci_cd/build-artifacts/temp2.tmp                 (text: “temp2”)
  /home/user/ci_cd/build-artifacts/cache/build123.tmp        (text: “cache build”)
  /home/user/ci_cd/build-artifacts/cache/old.build.tmp       (text: “old build”)
  /home/user/ci_cd/build-artifacts/important.bin             (binary placeholder)

No /home/user/cleanup directory exists initially.

Required final state after the agent finishes
---------------------------------------------
1. The four files listed above whose names end in “.tmp” must no longer be present on disk.  
2. /home/user/ci_cd/build-artifacts/important.bin must still exist and remain unchanged.  
3. A new directory /home/user/cleanup must now exist (mode 755 is fine).  
4. A new file /home/user/cleanup/deleted_tmp_files.log must exist and be readable by the current user.  

Exact contents of /home/user/cleanup/deleted_tmp_files.log that the grader will compare against:

/home/user/ci_cd/build-artifacts/cache/build123.tmp
/home/user/ci_cd/build-artifacts/cache/old.build.tmp
/home/user/ci_cd/build-artifacts/temp1.tmp
/home/user/ci_cd/build-artifacts/temp2.tmp

(An optional single trailing newline will be ignored by the grader.)

Nothing else should be created, deleted or modified.
